parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the shared `manifest.py` helper PLUS the `kbu subproject` subcommand group per PRD `kbu-start-v1` (agent-io/prds/kbu-start-v1/fullprompt.md). Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #4, #5, #6, #7, #8, #32, #33, #34, #36, #42, #44.

Scope — TWO new modules + their tests:

**1. `src/kbutillib/cli/manifest.py` — shared TOML helper (used by subproject, new-project, update, session, notebook)**:
   - `read_project_manifest(project_root: Path) -> dict` / `write_project_manifest(project_root, data)` for `kbu-project.toml` per the schema in PRD Implementation Decisions (sections `[project]`, `[[project.authors]]`, `[kbutillib]`, `[update]`, `[update.file_hashes]`).
   - `read_subproject_manifest(project_root, subproject_name) -> dict` / `write_subproject_manifest(project_root, subproject_name, data)` for `subprojects/<name>/kbu-subproject.toml` per the round-2 corrected schema (`[artifacts.notebooks]` REMOVED; `[[notebooks]]` array is single source of truth).
   - `now_utc_iso() -> str` returns ISO-8601 UTC with `Z` suffix.
   - `sha256_file(path: Path) -> str` returns hex digest (used by update.file_hashes).
   - `append_session_ref(project_root, subproject_name, ref)` / `append_notebook_entry_or_update(project_root, subproject_name, path, last_run_at)` for partial-write helpers that preserve all other fields.

**2. `src/kbutillib/cli/subproject.py`** — imports manifest.py for TOML I/O:
   - State-machine transition table (states: plan, p-review, build, b-review, run, synthesize, s-review, complete) and forward-precondition validator per the FULL precondition table in Implementation Decisions › Folded from confront round 2.
   - Verdict comment parser for `REVIEW_<stage>_<n>.md` (greps `<!-- kbu-review:verdict: pass|fail -->` at file top).
   - Subcommands: `create <name> [--title]`, `list`, `status <name>`, `advance <name> [--reverse]`, `set-status <name> <state>`.

3. Modify `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` to register the `subproject` subcommand group via the existing CLI entry pattern (match `init-notebook`, `jobs`, `jobdaemon` style).
4. Output schemas — `kbu subproject list` default TSV: header row `name<TAB>status<TAB>next_action`, recent-first by `created_at`; `--json` returns list of dicts.
5. Disabled-state reason strings: when `advance` fails, use exact strings `wrong-state`, `missing-artifact`, `no-subprojects`, `notebooks-stale`, `review-pending`.
6. Manifest key is `[subproject].status`. Allowed values exhaustively: plan, p-review, build, b-review, run, synthesize, s-review, complete. All timestamps ISO-8601 UTC with `Z` suffix.
7. Create `tests/cli/conftest.py` with fixtures: `tmp_kbu_project` (creates a temp directory with kbu-project.toml seeded via manifest.py), `tmp_subproject` (uses tmp_kbu_project + creates one subproject).
8. Create `tests/cli/test_manifest.py` exercising: round-trip preservation for both manifest types; append_session_ref preserves other fields; append_notebook_entry_or_update updates in place vs appends; sha256_file matches stdlib `hashlib.sha256`.
9. Create `tests/cli/test_subproject.py` exercising: each forward transition success + precondition rejection; reverse transition from each review state; set-status bypass; verdict parser handles pass/fail/missing-comment; list/status TSV + JSON formats.

Constraints:
- Use `tomllib` for read (Python 3.11+) and `tomli_w` for write. Add `tomli-w` to pyproject.toml if not already present.
- DO NOT add any AIAssistant imports here — manifest + subproject are self-contained.
- DO NOT touch any skill files or templates/ — downstream phases.
- Subsequent CLI phases (p2-p5) MUST import TOML helpers from manifest.py — do not duplicate the I/O logic.

Deliverables: `src/kbutillib/cli/manifest.py`, `src/kbutillib/cli/subproject.py`, modified `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py`, `tests/cli/conftest.py`, `tests/cli/test_manifest.py`, `tests/cli/test_subproject.py`. `pytest tests/cli/test_manifest.py tests/cli/test_subproject.py -v` passes.
parallel_group.success_criteria: pytest tests/cli/test_manifest.py tests/cli/test_subproject.py -v passes. `kbu subproject --help` lists create, list, status, advance, set-status subcommands. `kbu subproject create foo` in an empty temp project creates `subprojects/foo/kbu-subproject.toml` with `status = "plan"`. `kbu subproject advance foo` with no RESEARCH_PLAN.md exits non-zero and stderr contains `missing-artifact`. Manifest schema uses `[subproject].status` (verified by `grep -c 'status =' subprojects/foo/kbu-subproject.toml` returns 1) and has no `[artifacts.notebooks]` field. manifest.py exists and exports `read_project_manifest`, `write_project_manifest`, `read_subproject_manifest`, `write_subproject_manifest`, `now_utc_iso`, `sha256_file`, `append_session_ref`, `append_notebook_entry_or_update`.

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the `kbu session` subcommand group per PRD `kbu-start-v1`. Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #10, #11, #12, #13, #14, #43.

Scope:
1. Create `src/kbutillib/cli/session.py` with:
   - `_detect_aiassistant()` — reads colon-separated env var `KBU_AIA_PATHS` (default `~/Dropbox/Projects/AIAssistant/state/sessions.db:~/Projects/AIAssistant/state/sessions.db`); returns the AIAssistant repo root (parent of state/) for the first existing sessions.db, or None.
   - `_route_save_local(payload, subproject)` — writes YAML to `subprojects/<subproject>/sessions/<UTC-timestamp>-<skill>.yaml`. Generate session_id as 8-char hex of uuid4.
   - `_route_save_aia(payload, subproject, aia_root)` — prepends `<aia_root>/src` to sys.path; `from assistant.state import save_session, get_recent_sessions`; constructs `project_id = f"kbu-{repo_basename}-{subproject}"` (repo_basename from `Path.cwd().name`); auto-registers via `from assistant.state.registry import update_project` (ImportError of update_project is non-fatal — log warning, skip registration); calls `save_session(...)` with payload keys exactly: `project_id`, `command`, `topics_discussed`, `decisions_made`, `work_submitted`, `next_steps`, `summary` (+ optional `started_at`, `ended_at`); returns session_id.
   - Subcommands: `save --skill <name> --subproject <name> --summary <s> [--topics <s>] [--decisions <s>] [--next-steps <s>] [--work-completed <s>] [--json -]`; `list [--subproject <name>] [--limit N=20] [--json]`; `show <id>`.
   - `list` default TSV header row `id<TAB>at<TAB>subproject<TAB>skill<TAB>summary`, recent-first by `at`; summary collapses tabs/newlines to single space and truncates to 120 chars with `…`. `--json` returns full payloads.
2. Modify `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` to register `session` subcommand group.
3. AIAssistant import-failure path: log via stderr warning naming the ImportError, fall back to local YAML. NEVER silently swallow.
4. Create `tests/cli/test_session.py` covering: detection with both default paths absent → local YAML; KBU_AIA_PATHS override with custom path; AIAssistant present (mock both Path.exists and the assistant.state module via `sys.modules` monkeypatch) → save_session called with correct payload; ImportError of `assistant.state.registry.update_project` → registration skipped, save still proceeds; list TSV + JSON formats; show by id.

Constraints:
- Use `pyyaml` for local YAML.
- For tests, monkeypatch `sys.modules['assistant.state']` and `sys.modules['assistant.state.registry']` with `types.SimpleNamespace` shims; do not require real AIAssistant import path.
- Reuse the `tests/cli/conftest.py` fixtures from Phase 1.

Deliverables: `src/kbutillib/cli/session.py`, modified `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py`, `tests/cli/test_session.py`. `pytest tests/cli/test_session.py -v` passes.
parallel_group.success_criteria: pytest tests/cli/test_session.py -v passes. `kbu session --help` lists save, list, show. `kbu session save --skill kbu-plan --subproject foo --summary 'hi'` writes a YAML file under `subprojects/foo/sessions/`. `kbu session list` prints a header row matching `^id\tat\tsubproject\tskill\tsummary$`. With `KBU_AIA_PATHS=/nonexistent`, session save still works locally. Test for AIAssistant routing successfully simulates the import + call without requiring real AIAssistant.

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the `kbu notebook` subcommand group per PRD `kbu-start-v1`. Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #9, #35, #36.

Scope:
1. Create `src/kbutillib/cli/notebook.py` with:
   - `list_notebooks()` — scans `subprojects/*/notebooks/*.ipynb`, reads each subproject's `kbu-subproject.toml` `[[notebooks]]` array for recorded `last_run_at`; computes `modified_since_run` by comparing notebook mtime vs `last_run_at`.
   - `mark_run(path)` — appends/updates the matching `[[notebooks]]` entry in the subproject manifest with `last_run_at = now_utc_iso()`. If the entry exists, update it in place (preserving entry ordering); if new, append.
   - `exec_notebook(path, allow_errors=False)` — kernel selection: `jupyter_client.kernelspec.find_kernel_specs()` matching `kbu-project.toml [project].name`; fallback `python3` with stderr warning. Per-cell timeout 600s (override via `KBU_NOTEBOOK_CELL_TIMEOUT` env var). Stop-on-error by default; `--allow-errors` continues. Backup notebook at `<path>.bak.<UTC-timestamp>.ipynb` before in-place write. Stream output truncated at 1 MiB per cell with warning footer. Uses `nbclient.NotebookClient`.
   - Subcommands: `list [--json]`, `mark-run <path>`, `exec <path> [--allow-errors]`.
2. Modify `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` to register `notebook` subcommand group.
3. Output schemas — `kbu notebook list` default TSV: header row `path<TAB>subproject<TAB>last_run_at<TAB>modified_since_run`; ordered by subproject creation time then notebook filename; `last_run_at` empty string if never run; `modified_since_run` literal `true` or `false`. `--json` returns list of full dicts.
4. After `exec` completes successfully, call `mark_run(path)` automatically.
5. Create `tests/cli/test_notebook.py` covering: list with fixture notebooks (multiple subprojects, mixed last_run state); mark-run round-trip preserves other manifest fields; exec with passing fixture notebook stops on error when allow_errors=False; exec with `--allow-errors` continues; backup file created with correct timestamp format; kernel fallback to `python3` when project kernel absent; cell timeout override via env var.

Constraints:
- Add `nbclient` and `jupyter_client` to pyproject.toml if not already present.
- For exec tests, use minimal in-memory notebooks (one cell with `1+1` for happy path, one cell with `raise ValueError("x")` for error path).
- Reuse Phase 1 conftest.py fixtures.

Deliverables: `src/kbutillib/cli/notebook.py`, modified `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py`, `tests/cli/test_notebook.py`. `pytest tests/cli/test_notebook.py -v` passes.
parallel_group.success_criteria: pytest tests/cli/test_notebook.py -v passes. `kbu notebook --help` lists list, mark-run, exec. `kbu notebook list` in a fixture project with one subproject containing two notebooks prints exactly 3 lines (header + 2 notebooks). `kbu notebook exec` on a fixture passing notebook writes a `.bak.<timestamp>.ipynb` and updates `last_run_at` in the subproject manifest. `KBU_NOTEBOOK_CELL_TIMEOUT=1` causes a `time.sleep(5)` cell to fail with a timeout-related error.

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement the `kbu init` and `kbu doctor` subcommands per PRD `kbu-start-v1`. Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #15, #16, #17, #26 (init portion), #39 (init portion), #40, #41.

Scope:
1. Create `src/kbutillib/cli/init.py` with:
   - Marker file at `~/.config/kbu/init_done.json` (respect `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` when set). Schema version 1 with fields: `version`, `initialized_at` (ISO-8601 UTC `Z`), `kbutillib_repo_path` (absolute), `kbutillib_commit` (40-char SHA from `git rev-parse HEAD` in KBUtilLib root), `venv_manager` (one of `"venvman"` or `".venv"`), `venv_python` (absolute path), `jupyter_kernel_name` (always `"kbutillib"` for tier-1 init).
   - `init()` — on macOS only (`sys.platform == "darwin"`) OR with `KBU_PLATFORM_OVERRIDE=force`: detect venvman via `shutil.which("venvman")`; if present run `venvman create --project kbutillib --dir <KBUTILLIB_ROOT> --python 3.11`; activation via the generated `activate.sh`; resolve venv python by reading the activate.sh-emitted VIRTUAL_ENV. If venvman fails non-zero, fall back to `python -m venv .venv` in KBUTILLIB_ROOT with a stderr warning naming the venvman error. If venvman absent, use `python -m venv .venv` directly. Then `<venv_python> -m pip install -e <KBUTILLIB_ROOT>` and `<venv_python> -m ipykernel install --user --name=kbutillib --display-name='KBUtilLib (kbu)'`. Write marker on success. Non-macOS without override: print the v1 macOS-only message specified in fullprompt.md and exit 1.
   - `init_status()` — return exit code per AC #17: 0 if marker present AND `venv_python` resolves to an executable; 1 if marker missing; 2 if marker present but `venv_python` no longer resolves.
   - `doctor()` — runs probes in order: (a) marker present (via init_status); (b) `cursor` on PATH (`shutil.which`); (c) Claude extension installed (`cursor --list-extensions | grep -i anthropic.claude-code` — best-effort; if cursor missing, mark this probe SKIP); (d) `kbu --version` resolves; (e) project venv kernel registered (parse `jupyter kernelspec list --json`). Output one line per probe: `[PASS|FAIL|SKIP] <probe-name>: <detail>`. Exit 0 if all PASS or SKIP, exit 1 if any FAIL.
   - Subcommands: `init [--status|--update]` and `doctor [--verbose]`. `init --update` runs `git -C <KBUTILLIB_ROOT> pull` (best-effort, warn on fail), then `<venv_python> -m pip install -e <KBUTILLIB_ROOT> --upgrade`.
2. Modify `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` to register `init` and `doctor` subcommands. (`init` and `doctor` are top-level, not nested.)
3. Create `tests/cli/test_init.py` covering: marker schema fields; venvman detection path with mocked `shutil.which`; venvman failure → fallback to `python -m venv` with warning; non-Darwin platform exits 1 unless override set; init --status exit codes (0/1/2); doctor probes (cursor present/absent, claude ext present/absent, kbu version, kernel registered).

Constraints:
- Mock `subprocess.run` for venvman + pip + ipykernel + git calls; don't actually create venvs in tests.
- Mock `shutil.which` for cursor / venvman / kbu detection.
- Mock `sys.platform` via monkeypatch.

Deliverables: `src/kbutillib/cli/init.py`, modified `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py`, `tests/cli/test_init.py`. `pytest tests/cli/test_init.py -v` passes.
parallel_group.success_criteria: pytest tests/cli/test_init.py -v passes. `kbu init --status` on a system without the marker exits 1. `kbu doctor` runs and prints one line per probe starting with `[PASS|FAIL|SKIP]`. Test that simulates non-Darwin platform without override confirms exit 1 with the macOS-only message. Test confirms venvman invocation uses exactly the form `venvman create --project kbutillib --dir <path> --python 3.11`.

parallel_group.prompt:
Implement `kbu new-project` and `kbu update` per PRD `kbu-start-v1`. Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #18, #19, #20, #21, #22, #23, #26 (new-project portion), #39 (new-project portion), #45.

Scope:

**`src/kbutillib/cli/new_project.py`**:
- `new_project(path, name, author, affiliation, orcid, first_subproject=None)`:
  1. Reject if `path` exists. Create `path` directory (and parents).
  2. Copy `templates/student-project/` tree verbatim to `path`. Substitute `{{project_name}}` in BOTH filenames (e.g., `{{project_name}}.code-workspace` → `<name>.code-workspace`) AND file contents.
  3. Platform gate: same as Phase 4 — macOS or `KBU_PLATFORM_OVERRIDE=force` only; otherwise exit 1 with v1 message before creating venv.
  4. Create per-project venv: venvman if `shutil.which("venvman")` else `python -m venv .venv` in `path`. Use exact venvman syntax: `venvman create --project <name> --dir <path> --python 3.11`. Fall back to `.venv` on venvman failure with stderr warning.
  5. In the venv python: `pip install -e <KBUTILLIB_ROOT>` (NOT vendored — read source path from the running `kbu`'s install location via `kbutillib.__file__`).
  6. Register Jupyter kernel: `<venv_python> -m ipykernel install --user --name=<name> --display-name='<name> (kbu)'`.
  7. Write `kbu-project.toml` with `[project] name`, `created_at` (ISO-8601 UTC Z), `[[project.authors]]` table with name/affiliation/orcid; `[kbutillib] source_path` (KBUTILLIB_ROOT absolute), `source_commit` (current commit SHA); `[update] last_pulled_at`, `last_pulled_commit`, `[update.file_hashes]` populated with SHA-256 of every file under copied `.claude/commands/` and `.vscode/`.
  8. `git init` + initial commit (`feat(kbu): initialize project via kbu new-project`).
  9. If `first_subproject` given, invoke `kbu subproject create <first_subproject>` in the new project.
  10. Print: `Open in Cursor: cursor <path>/<name>.code-workspace` and `Then in Cursor: open terminal → run claude → type /kbu-start`.
- Subcommand: `new-project <path> [--name NAME] [--author NAME] [--affiliation AFF] [--orcid ID] [--first-subproject NAME]`. If args missing, prompt via stdin (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — this is CLI).

**`src/kbutillib/cli/update.py`**:
- `update(set_source=None, check=False, yes=False)`:
  1. If `--check` and `--yes` both passed: exit non-zero with error.
  2. Read `kbu-project.toml` from `Path.cwd()`.
  3. If `set_source`: write `[kbutillib].source_path = set_source` and clear `[update].last_pulled_commit`; rewrite toml; exit 0.
  4. Resolve source: `Path(cfg['kbutillib']['source_path'])`. If absent on disk, exit with `Parent KBUtilLib not found at <source>. Run \`kbu update --set-source <new-path>\`.`.
  5. If `(source/'.git').is_dir()`: run `git -C <source> pull` (best-effort; warn on fail).
  6. Compute current source commit via `git -C <source> rev-parse HEAD`.
  7. Build diff: walk `<source>/templates/student-project/.claude/commands/` and `<source>/templates/student-project/.vscode/` for current files; compare each against the recorded `[update.file_hashes]` AND against the `last_pulled_commit` git state via `git -C <source> show <sha>:<relpath>`.
  8. Construct `TemplateDiff` list (path, status='added'|'modified'|'deleted', old_hash, new_hash) where hashes are SHA-256 hex.
  9. Locally-modified detection: for each tracked file, compute current SHA-256 of the file in the CURRENT student project (under `Path.cwd()`) and compare against `[update.file_hashes][<relpath>]`. Mismatch → `locally_modified`.
  10. If `--check`: print diff summary; return 0.
  11. Display summary. If any `locally_modified` files would be overwritten by the diff AND not `--yes`: prompt `Overwrite locally-modified files? (y/n)`. Abort on `n`.
  12. Apply diff (copy from source to cwd), update `[update.last_pulled_at]`, `[update.last_pulled_commit]`, and recompute `[update.file_hashes]` for all tracked paths in their NEW (post-update) state.
- Subcommand: `update [--set-source PATH] [--check] [--yes]`.

Also modify `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py` to register both `new-project` and `update`.

**Tests** — `tests/cli/test_new_project.py` and `tests/cli/test_update.py`:
- new_project: template substitution in filenames + content; venv creation mocked; kbu-project.toml correct schema; file_hashes computed for all tracked files; first_subproject creates subproject; rejects existing target; non-Darwin exits 1 without override.
- update: --set-source clears last_pulled_commit and rewrites toml; missing source path errors; --check is dry-run; --check + --yes errors; locally-modified detection (modify a tracked file post-pull, ensure clobber-prompt fires); --yes bypasses prompt; post-apply file_hashes recomputed.

Constraints:
- Mock subprocess for venvman / pip / git / ipykernel.
- Use temp dirs for source + target.
- Reuse Phase 1 conftest.py.

Deliverables: `src/kbutillib/cli/new_project.py`, `src/kbutillib/cli/update.py`, modified `src/kbutillib/cli/__init__.py`, `tests/cli/test_new_project.py`, `tests/cli/test_update.py`. `pytest tests/cli/test_new_project.py tests/cli/test_update.py -v` passes.
parallel_group.success_criteria: pytest tests/cli/test_new_project.py tests/cli/test_update.py -v passes. `kbu new-project /tmp/foo --name foo --author Test --affiliation X --orcid 0` (with mocks) creates `/tmp/foo/kbu-project.toml` with required sections and writes `[update.file_hashes]`. `kbu update --check --yes` exits non-zero with mutually-exclusive error. `kbu update --set-source /new/path` rewrites the toml and clears `last_pulled_commit`. Test that modifies a tracked template file in cwd then runs update without --yes confirms an interactive prompt would fire.

parallel_group.prompt:
Create the `templates/student-project/` tree per PRD `kbu-start-v1` Implementation Decisions › Template tree section. Satisfies Acceptance Criterion #7 (root manifest schema) and #31 (repo-root-relative paths). Skill bodies are deferred to Phase 7 — this task only creates the structural skeleton + non-skill files.

Scope:
1. Create directory tree under `KBUtilLib/templates/student-project/`:
   - `.claude/commands/` directory (empty placeholder — skill files added in Phase 7)
   - `.vscode/extensions.json`
   - `{{project_name}}.code-workspace`
   - `subprojects/.gitkeep`
   - `.gitignore`
   - `README.md`
   - `kbu-project.toml.template`
2. `.vscode/extensions.json` content: JSON object with `recommendations: ["anthropic.claude-code"]`.
3. `{{project_name}}.code-workspace` content: VS Code multi-root workspace JSON with `folders: [{ "path": "." }]`, `settings: {}`. The `{{project_name}}` token in the filename is substituted by `kbu new-project`.
4. `.gitignore` content: standard Python ignore (`.venv/`, `__pycache__/`, `*.egg-info/`, `.ipynb_checkpoints/`, `.DS_Store`) + `sessions/` (local YAMLs are not committed by default) + `*.bak.*.ipynb` (notebook exec backups) + `.kbcache/`.
5. `README.md` content: student-facing quickstart, 1 page max. Sections: `# {{project_name}}` (substituted by new-project), `## Quick start` (one numbered list: source activate.sh, run `claude`, type `/kbu-start`, follow the menu), `## Subproject layout` (the canonical subproject tree from the PRD), `## State machine` (one-line description of the 8 states), `## Updating` (one line referencing `/kbu-start` → Update). Include `{{project_name}}` token to be substituted by new-project.
6. `kbu-project.toml.template` content: the EXACT schema from PRD Implementation Decisions (project, project.authors, kbutillib, update sections). Use `{{project_name}}`, `{{author_name}}`, `{{author_affiliation}}`, `{{author_orcid}}`, `{{kbutillib_source_path}}`, `{{kbutillib_source_commit}}`, `{{created_at}}` as substitution tokens. `new_project.py` (Phase 5) populates them.
7. Place placeholder TODO files in `.claude/commands/`:
   - `.claude/commands/kbu-start.md` containing only `<!-- TODO: tier-2 dashboard skill, filled in Phase 7 -->`
   - `.claude/commands/kbu-plan.md`, `kbu-build.md`, `kbu-run.md`, `kbu-synthesize.md`, `kbu-review.md`, `kbu-literature-review.md`, `kbu-diagnose.md`, `kbu-update.md` — each with `<!-- TODO: filled in Phase 7 -->`.
   The placeholders let `kbu new-project` succeed end-to-end in Phase 5 tests; real content arrives in Phase 7.

Constraints:
- Write all paths repo-root-relative — NO literal nested `KBUtilLib/templates/...` folder.
- Validate `.vscode/extensions.json` parses as JSON.
- Validate `kbu-project.toml.template` parses as TOML AFTER token substitution (do a substitution test with dummy values to confirm).

Deliverable: complete `templates/student-project/` tree as specified. `find templates/student-project -type f | sort` matches the expected file list. `python -c 'import json; json.load(open("templates/student-project/.vscode/extensions.json"))'` succeeds.
parallel_group.success_criteria: `find templates/student-project -type f | sort` lists the 12 expected files (vscode + workspace + subprojects/.gitkeep + .gitignore + README + kbu-project.toml.template + 9 skill placeholder files). `python -c 'import json; json.load(open("templates/student-project/.vscode/extensions.json"))'` succeeds and recommendations contains 'anthropic.claude-code'. Substituting dummy tokens into kbu-project.toml.template and parsing via tomllib succeeds.

parallel_group.prompt:
Write the tier-1 `/kbu-start` skill at `KBUtilLib/.claude/commands/kbu-start.md` per PRD `kbu-start-v1`. Satisfies Acceptance Criterion #1.

Scope:
1. Create `KBUtilLib/.claude/commands/kbu-start.md` with this exact menu (status-aware via `kbu init --status`):
   - **Help** — single-screen explainer: what KBUtilLib is, the tier-2 workflow it produces (plan → review → build → review → run → synthesize → review → complete), where to read more.
   - **Initialize** — runs `kbu init` (idempotent); calls `cursor-setup` skill for Cursor + extension detection; shown only when `kbu init --status` returns non-zero (else greys out as `(not available: already-initialized)` — extending the dashboard reason vocabulary just for tier-1).
   - **New project** — collects via AskUserQuestion: project name (snake_case), target path (default `~/<name>`), author name, affiliation, ORCID, optional first subproject name. Invokes `kbu new-project <path> --name <name> --author <name> --affiliation <aff> --orcid <orcid> [--first-subproject <name>]`. After return, prints the Cursor + claude instructions verbatim.
   - **Update** — invokes `kbu init --update` to pull KBUtilLib master + reinstall editable.
2. Slash-command frontmatter style — match existing `KBUtilLib/.claude/commands/kbutillib-expert.md` shape (read it first to align on format conventions used in this repo).
3. Provenance header at top of file (this is a NEW skill, not a fork — header just records `kbu skill provenance / type: net-new / created: <YYYY-MM-DD>`).
4. The skill must work without ClaudeCommands installed. Do not reference `claude-skills sync` or any AIAssistant tooling.
5. Do not implement state-machine logic in the skill prose — defer all of that to the tier-2 (Phase 7.2). Tier-1's job is machine-level setup + new-project bootstrap.

parallel_group.prompt:
Write the 3 net-new skill files under `KBUtilLib/templates/student-project/.claude/commands/`, replacing Phase 6 placeholders. Satisfies Acceptance Criteria #2, #3 (partial), #37 (net-new header), #44.

Files: `kbu-start.md` (tier-2 dashboard), `kbu-run.md`, `kbu-update.md`.

**1. `kbu-start.md` (tier-2 dashboard)** — status-aware navigation.
   - On invocation: run `kbu subproject list` AND `kbu session list --limit 5` (prefer `--json` for parse stability); render both as tables.
   - AskUserQuestion menu with these items: Help / Plan / Build / Run / Synthesize / Review / Literature review / Diagnose / Update.
   - Each item gates against subproject state (queried from `kbu subproject status <name>`). Disabled items use one of the exact reason strings: `wrong-state`, `missing-artifact`, `no-subprojects`, `notebooks-stale`, `review-pending`. Format: `Run (not available: wrong-state)`.
   - On pick, route to the corresponding slash command (`/kbu-plan`, `/kbu-build`, etc).

**2. `kbu-run.md`** — deliberate notebook runner.
   - Precondition: subproject state `run`.
   - Run `kbu notebook list --json`; present a menu of notebooks across subprojects (path + last_run_at + modified_since_run).
   - On pick: run `kbu notebook exec <path>`. Narrate per-cell output concisely (≤1 paragraph per cell).
   - After exec: `kbu notebook list` will show modified_since_run=false. When ALL notebooks in the subproject have last_run_at set AND modified_since_run=false, tell the user the subproject can now advance to synthesize (invoke `kbu subproject advance <name>` followed by `/kbu-synthesize`).
   - End: `kbu session save --skill kbu-run --subproject <name> --summary "..."`.

**3. `kbu-update.md`** — wraps `kbu update` CLI.
   - Run `kbu update --check` (dry run) to get the diff summary.
   - Present the summary to the student.
   - Ask confirmation (AskUserQuestion).
   - On confirm: run `kbu update --yes` to apply (bypasses interactive clobber-warn since we just got confirmation here).
   - Report what changed.
   - End: session save.

Provenance header on ALL THREE files (canonical shape for net-new):
```
<!--
kbu skill provenance
type: net-new
created: 2026-06-05
-->
```

Constraints:
- Each skill file ≤ 300 lines.
- No file imports `assistant.state` or references AIAssistant runtime.
- No file references `register_prd`, `maestro submit`, `agentforge`, or any AgentForge / Maestro / Conductor concept.
- kbu-start dashboard MUST contain all 5 disabled-item reason strings somewhere in its prose (so the student dashboard's grep-test passes).
